steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to emphasize the historical significance of Manuel Reyes’s enlistment date. The decisive clue in the notes is that only a small number of Filipino-descent sailors served in the Civil War at all — placing Reyes within that rare group is what makes his date significant.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Both Manuel Reyes and Rafael Santos were US Navy sailors of Filipino descent, but Reyes enlisted in the Navy in 1863, two years later than Santos.: This compares Reyes’s date to Santos’s date but never explains why either date matters historically — later does not mean significant. ✗
  • Choice B — Manuel Reyes was a US Navy sailor of Filipino descent, along with Rafael Santos, who was born in the Philippines around 1834.: This mentions Santos’s birth year instead of Reyes’s enlistment date and provides no connection to the Civil War’s historical context. ✗
  • Choice C — Manuel Reyes enlisted in the US Navy in 1863, making him one of the few sailors of Filipino descent to serve in the US Civil War (1861–1865).: This gives the enlistment date and directly connects it to the rarity established by the notes — exactly the historical significance the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — When Manuel Reyes enlisted in the US Navy in November 1863, he joined sailors such as Rafael Santos, who had been born in the Philippines around 1834.: This provides Reyes’s enlistment date but frames it only as joining another sailor, not as belonging to a historically rare group. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
